narrative.summary:
📍 The Crosby Bar
79 Crosby St, New York, NY 10012, USA

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're in the cobblestone stretch of Soho where the boutiques thin out. Inside the Crosby hotel, the bar pulls you past the lobby with its velvet banquettes and those whimsical dog portraits on the walls—greyhounds and terriers rendered like aristocratic ancestors. The light stays dim even at lunch, the kind of place where rosé at 3pm makes perfect sense.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The kitchen riffs on French brasserie standards with enough tweaks to keep you interested—their French onion soup comes spiked with tarragon instead of the usual thyme-heavy approach. Truffle fries arrive properly crisp, and afternoon tea runs the full tiered service without rushing you out the door. Expect $20-30 for appetizers, more if you commit to the full tea spread.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Too hotel with a chic and charming bar and dining area. Sat at the bar and had a dedicated server who was on the move to serve his guests. Enjoyed truffle fries, Rose and a non-traditional French onion soup.  It had a hint of tarragon and was delicious. Loved the design with dogs motif through the hotel lobby."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I love tea time. I try to search this out whenever I travel. The Crosby did not disappoint. Stunning decor, friendly and attentive wait staff, Central location. Also, they did not rush us out! I did a few afternoon teas during this trip to New York and this was the best experience."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Make reservations because it gets very busy. Came here for tea party service and loved it. The sandwiches and desserts were delicious. I had mint tea while my friends had the hot chocolate. Which they both said was thick hot coco. Will come again"
